name: PhoneInput
description: International phone number input with country selector (flag + dial code + chevron), clear button, and optional label, hint, and error. Extends HTML input attributes (except size and type).

props.name: error
props.description: Error message — puts the field in error state.

props.name: country
props.description: Selected country object with code, dialCode, flag, and name.

props.name: onCountryClick
props.description: Callback when the country selector is clicked.

props.name: onClear
props.description: Callback when clear button is clicked.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the root element. Layout composition only — do NOT use for custom styling.

states.rationale: PhoneInput hover changes border color only — no background OKLCH shift or overlay. Same token-swap pattern as InputField.

keyboard.Tab: Move focus between country button, input, and clear button

propertyMapping.note: Visual-only Figma property. In code, states come from CSS pseudo-classes (:hover, :focus-within) and data attributes (data-error, data-disabled).
Figma 'text' maps to the HTML input 'value' attribute passed through PhoneInputProps.
HTML attribute pass-through. Default '000-000-0000' in both Figma and code.
Figma 'code' is a string (e.g. '+1'). In code, dial code is part of the CountryOption object: country.dialCode.
Figma 'error' is a boolean toggling the error row. In code, 'error' is a string — truthy = error state + message.
Figma 'errorText' content maps to the React 'error' string prop value.

useWhen: International phone number entry with country selection
Forms requiring phone numbers with dial code
Contact or registration forms with phone fields
doNotUseFor: Phone display only — render text directly

knownDeviations.description: Figma 'phone' component does not include label or hint rows. In code, PhoneInput renders label, field box, and hint/error as a single component. Label and hint are code-only props.
Figma has a simple 'code' string property for dial code and a built-in US flag. In code, country is a full CountryOption object { code, dialCode, flag, name } supporting any country with flag emoji.
